11.label: Down the Rabbit-Hole
11.recap: Bored by the riverbank, Alice follows a waistcoated White Rabbit down a hole and lands in a hall of locked doors.

11.label: The Pool of Tears
11.recap: After shooting up tall and shrinking small, Alice weeps a whole pool of tears and swims off through it.

11.label: A Caucus-Race and a Long Tale
11.recap: A soggy crowd of birds and animals dries off with a muddled Caucus-Race, then drifts away one by one.

11.label: The Rabbit Sends in a Little Bill
11.recap: Mistaken for the Rabbit's maid, Alice grows enormous inside his house before nibbling her way back out and fleeing.

11.label: Advice from a Caterpillar
11.recap: A hookah-smoking Caterpillar quizzes Alice on who she is, then leaves her a mushroom that changes her size.

11.label: Pig and Pepper
11.recap: In a pepper-filled kitchen Alice meets the Duchess, finds the grinning Cheshire Cat, and rescues a baby that turns into a pig.

11.label: A Mad Tea-Party
11.recap: The Hatter, the March Hare, and a drowsy Dormouse pull Alice into a riddling, never-ending tea-party.

11.label: The Queen's Croquet-Ground
11.recap: Gardeners frantically paint white roses red as the fierce Queen of Hearts sweeps Alice into a chaotic croquet match.

11.label: The Mock Turtle's Story
11.recap: The Duchess waxes on about morals, then a wistful Mock Turtle and a Gryphon recount their odd schooling by the sea.

11.label: The Lobster Quadrille
11.recap: The Mock Turtle and Gryphon dance the Lobster Quadrille and coax Alice through some thoroughly muddled recitations.

11.label: Who Stole the Tarts?
11.recap: At the trial of the Knave of Hearts, witnesses give nonsense testimony while Alice quietly begins to grow again.

11.label: Alice's Evidence
11.recap: Alice towers over the court, scatters the Queen's nonsense, and wakes on the riverbank to find it was all a dream.

35.label: The Machine
35.recap: At his dinner party the Time Traveller shows a small glittering machine and sends it vanishing into time before his startled guests.

35.label: Time Travelling
35.recap: Returning dishevelled a week later, the Traveller tells how he first hurtled forward through the centuries.

35.label: In the Golden Age
35.recap: He arrives in the year 802,701 among the gentle, childlike Eloi in a soft and crumbling paradise.

35.label: The Sunset of Mankind
35.recap: Puzzling over the frail Eloi, the Traveller discovers his time machine has been dragged away and hidden.

35.label: The Morlocks
35.recap: He uncovers the pale, underground Morlocks and the grim truth of how the two races of the future now live.

35.label: The Palace of Green Porcelain
35.recap: Hunting for a weapon, the Traveller and his Eloi friend Weena explore a vast ruined museum of the past.

35.label: The Trap of the White Sphinx
35.recap: Fighting the Morlocks in the dark to reach his machine, the Traveller loses Weena in the chaos.

35.label: The Further Vision
35.recap: Escaping forward in time, he travels on to a dying earth beneath a vast, cold, red sun.

35.label: The Traveller's Return
35.recap: He races home to his laboratory and his waiting guests, exhausted and forever changed by what he has seen.

43.label: Story of the Door
43.recap: Lawyer Utterson hears how a cold, brutal man named Hyde trampled a child and paid the family off with a cheque signed by the respected Dr Jekyll.

43.label: Search for Mr. Hyde
43.recap: Troubled by Jekyll's will leaving everything to Hyde, Utterson haunts the door until he finally meets the chilling man face to face.

43.label: Dr. Jekyll Was Quite at Ease
43.recap: Over dinner Jekyll airily waves off Utterson's worries, asking only that, whatever should happen, Hyde be looked after.

43.label: The Carew Murder Case
43.recap: Hyde beats a kindly old gentleman to death in the street, and all London turns out in a manhunt for him.

43.label: Incident of the Letter
43.recap: Hyde has vanished. A pale, shaken Jekyll shows Utterson a farewell letter from Hyde, but its handwriting plants a fresh doubt.

43.label: Incident of Dr. Lanyon
43.recap: With Hyde gone Jekyll grows sociable again, then suddenly shuts himself away, while old Dr Lanyon sickens under some secret dread and dies.

43.label: Incident at the Window
43.recap: Chatting up at his window, Jekyll's face twists into such horror that Utterson and Enfield flee the courtyard in silence.

43.label: The Last Night
43.recap: Jekyll's butler Poole comes in terror: a stranger's voice is locked in the cabinet, and the two men resolve to break down the door.

43.label: Dr. Lanyon's Narrative
43.recap: Lanyon's sealed letter reveals what he saw that night: a potion drunk, and a transformation so impossible it shattered him.

43.label: Henry Jekyll's Full Statement
43.recap: In his own hand, Jekyll confesses the whole truth of his double life, and how it finally consumed him.

46.label: Marley's Ghost
46.recap: On Christmas Eve the miser Scrooge sneers at the season, until his dead partner Marley's chained ghost warns that three spirits will visit him.

46.label: The First of the Spirits
46.recap: The Ghost of Christmas Past carries Scrooge through his lonely boyhood, a kind old employer, and the sweetheart he lost to greed.

46.label: The Second of the Spirits
46.recap: The Ghost of Christmas Present reveals the warm, poor Cratchit home and frail Tiny Tim, and the world's mingled joy and want.

46.label: The Last of the Spirits
46.recap: A silent, hooded phantom shows Scrooge a death no one mourns, and lets him read his own name upon a neglected grave.

46.label: The End of It
46.recap: Scrooge wakes on Christmas morning overjoyed and made new, and keeps Christmas generously for the rest of his days.

55.label: The Cyclone
55.recap: A cyclone carries Dorothy, her dog Toto, and the whole farmhouse far away from the grey Kansas prairie.

55.label: The Munchkins
55.recap: The house lands on the Wicked Witch of the East; the grateful Munchkins give Dorothy silver shoes and send her to the Wizard for help getting home.

55.label: The Scarecrow
55.recap: On the yellow brick road Dorothy frees a friendly Scarecrow who wishes only for brains.

55.label: The Tin Woodman
55.recap: They free a rusted Tin Woodman who longs above all for a heart.

55.label: The Cowardly Lion
55.recap: A great but timid Lion joins the company, hoping the Wizard can grant him courage.

55.label: The Poppy Field
55.recap: A field of deadly poppies nearly claims Dorothy and the Lion before the others carry them to safety.

55.label: The Emerald City
55.recap: In the dazzling Emerald City the Wizard appears differently to each, and will help only if they destroy the Wicked Witch of the West.

55.label: The Wicked Witch
55.recap: The Witch sets wolves, crows, and winged monkeys on the travelers, then captures Dorothy as a slave.

55.label: The Witch Melts
55.recap: Dorothy melts the Wicked Witch with a bucket of water and frees her friends and the Winged Monkeys.

55.label: The Man Behind the Curtain
55.recap: Back in the city they discover the great and terrible Oz is only an ordinary humbug hiding behind a curtain.

55.label: Wishes Granted
55.recap: The humbug Wizard cleverly gives the Scarecrow, the Woodman, and the Lion the brains, heart, and courage they craved.

55.label: Home Again
55.recap: Glinda reveals the silver shoes can carry Dorothy anywhere, and she returns at last to Kansas and Aunt Em.

74.label: Tom in Trouble
74.recap: Mischievous orphan Tom dodges his Aunt Polly, skips school for a swim, and scraps with a newcomer.

74.label: Tom Meets Becky
74.recap: Dreading school, Tom is smitten at first sight by the new girl in town, Becky Thatcher.

74.label: Murder in the Graveyard
74.recap: Sneaking to the graveyard at midnight, Tom and Huck witness Injun Joe murder the doctor and frame poor Muff Potter.

74.label: Off to be Pirates
74.recap: Heartsick over Becky, Tom runs off with Huck and Joe to live as pirates on Jackson's Island.

74.label: Their Own Funeral
74.recap: Given up for drowned, the boys creep back to town and stroll in on their own funeral, to everyone's joy.

74.label: Tom Takes the Blame
74.recap: At school Tom nobly takes Becky's whipping to spare her from disgrace.

74.label: The Trial
74.recap: At Muff Potter's murder trial Tom bravely testifies to the truth, and Injun Joe leaps from the window and escapes.

74.label: Hunting Treasure
74.recap: Tom and Huck go hunting for buried treasure and end up creeping through a haunted house.

74.label: Injun Joe's Gold
74.recap: In the haunted house the boys watch Injun Joe dig up a box of gold and carry it off to a secret hiding place.

74.label: Lost in the Cave
74.recap: Tom and Becky lose their way deep in McDougal's Cave, where Injun Joe is also hiding.

74.label: Injun Joe's End
74.recap: After Tom and Becky's rescue the cave is sealed, and Injun Joe is found dead inside it.

74.label: Rich and Respectable
74.recap: Tom and Huck recover the hidden gold, become the town's heroes, and the Widow Douglas takes Huck in.

76.label: Civilizing Huck
76.recap: Adopted by the Widow Douglas and schooled by Miss Watson, restless Huck slips out for midnight adventures with Tom Sawyer.

76.label: Pap Returns
76.recap: Huck's drunken father reappears to grab his money and shuts Huck away in a lonely riverside cabin.

76.label: The Escape
76.recap: Huck fakes his own murder to be free of Pap and slips away down the river to Jackson's Island.

76.label: Jim on the Island
76.recap: On the island Huck finds Jim, who has run off to escape being sold away from his family.

76.label: Down the River
76.recap: Disguised as a girl, Huck learns men are hunting Jim, and the two push off down the Mississippi on a raft.

76.label: Lost in the Fog
76.recap: A thick fog parts Huck and Jim on the water, and Huck's cruel trick afterward shames him into apologizing to his friend.

76.label: The Feud
76.recap: Taken in by the genteel Grangerfords, Huck is swept into their bloody feud with the Shepherdsons.

76.label: The Duke and the King
76.recap: Two con men styling themselves a duke and a king commandeer the raft and pull Huck and Jim into their swindles.

76.label: The Wilks Fraud
76.recap: The pair pose as the long-lost brothers of the dead Mr Wilks to rob his trusting orphaned daughters.

76.label: Huck's Choice
76.recap: After the con men sell Jim, Huck wrestles with his conscience and resolves to free his friend, whatever it costs his soul.

76.label: At the Phelps Farm
76.recap: Mistaken for Tom Sawyer at the farm where Jim is held, Huck is joined by the real Tom and a wild rescue scheme.

76.label: Setting Jim Free
76.recap: Tom's needlessly elaborate escape goes wrong and he is shot, but the truth that Jim is already free finally comes out.

84.label: Walton's Letters
84.recap: Explorer Robert Walton writes home from the edge of the Arctic, longing for a true friend as his ship pushes north.

84.label: A Stranger on the Ice
84.recap: Walton's crew rescues a wasted, haunted traveller from the ice, Victor Frankenstein, who begins to tell his tale.

84.label: Victor's Childhood
84.recap: Victor recalls a happy Geneva boyhood and his beloved adopted sister Elizabeth.

84.label: The Spark of Life
84.recap: At university Victor masters natural philosophy and grows obsessed with discovering the secret of life itself.

84.label: The Creature Wakes
84.recap: On a dreary November night Victor gives his creation life, then flees in horror at the thing he has made.

84.label: News of a Death
84.recap: A letter from home brings terrible news: Victor's young brother William has been murdered.

84.label: Justine's Trial
84.recap: The gentle servant Justine is tried and condemned for William's death, though Victor dreads who is truly to blame.

84.label: Meeting on the Glacier
84.recap: High on a mountain glacier the creature finds Victor and demands, at last, to be heard.

84.label: The Creature's Tale
84.recap: The creature recounts its lonely awakening and how, unseen, it slowly learned to feel and to survive.

84.label: Rejected by All
84.recap: After secretly learning tenderness from a poor family, the creature is driven off in terror the moment it is seen.

84.label: A Terrible Bargain
84.recap: The creature demands that Victor build him a mate, swearing to vanish from mankind forever in return.

84.label: The Promise Broken
84.recap: Sickened by his task, Victor destroys the half-finished second creature, and his enemy vows a terrible revenge.

84.label: The Pursuit North
84.recap: All he loved now taken from him, Victor chases the creature across the frozen north, back to where Walton found him.

98.label: The Period
98.recap: In 1775 England and France teeter on the edge of upheaval as the story opens.

98.label: Recalled to Life
98.recap: At Dover, Mr Lorry of Tellson's Bank gently tells young Lucie Manette that the father she thought dead is alive.

98.label: The Shoemaker
98.recap: In Paris the Defarges lead Lucie to her broken father, kept making shoes in a garret after long years in the Bastille.

98.label: A Disappointment
98.recap: Charles Darnay's London treason trial collapses when the lawyer Sydney Carton points out his uncanny resemblance to the accused.

98.label: Hundreds of People
98.recap: At the Manettes' tranquil Soho home a circle of admirers gathers around the gentle Lucie.

98.label: The Gorgon's Head
98.recap: In France the cruel Marquis is found stabbed in his bed, a note left by the people he has trampled.

98.label: Two Promises
98.recap: Darnay asks Doctor Manette for Lucie's hand, promising to reveal his true name on the wedding morning.

98.label: A Fellow of No Delicacy
98.recap: The wasted Sydney Carton confesses his hopeless love to Lucie and vows he would give his life for anyone she loves.

98.label: Echoing Footsteps
98.recap: As happy years pass for Lucie in London, far off in Paris the storming of the Bastille begins.

98.label: Drawn to the Loadstone Rock
98.recap: A desperate plea draws Darnay back to revolutionary Paris, straight into danger.

98.label: In Secret
98.recap: Darnay reaches Paris only to be seized as an emigrant aristocrat and thrown into prison.

98.label: Triumph
98.recap: Hauled before the Revolutionary Tribunal, Darnay is freed thanks to Doctor Manette, only to be seized again.

98.label: The Game Made
98.recap: Carton uncovers Madame Defarge's deadly designs and quietly begins a plan of his own.

120.label: The Old Sea-Dog
120.recap: Young Jim Hawkins recalls the scarred old buccaneer who lodged at his family's inn and dreaded a one-legged man.

120.label: Black Dog Appears
120.recap: A sinister visitor named Black Dog confronts the old captain, and the shock brings on a fit.

120.label: The Black Spot
120.recap: A blind beggar delivers the dreaded black spot, and the terror of it strikes the captain down.

120.label: The Sea-Chest
120.recap: With the captain dead and danger closing in, Jim and his mother open the sea-chest and snatch a packet.

120.label: The Captain's Papers
120.recap: The packet proves to hold a map to Flint's buried treasure, and the squire vows to fit out a ship.

120.label: I Go to Bristol
120.recap: Jim joins the venture and meets the genial one-legged ship's cook, Long John Silver.

120.label: The Voyage
120.recap: The Hispaniola puts to sea, though uneasy currents already run among the crew.

120.label: Council of War
120.recap: As the island is sighted Jim shares what he overheard hidden in the apple barrel: the crew means mutiny.

120.label: My Shore Adventure
120.recap: The ship anchors off the gloomy island and the mutiny breaks into the open.

120.label: The Man of the Island
120.recap: Ashore and alone, Jim meets Ben Gunn, a sailor long marooned and desperate to get home.

120.label: My Sea Adventure
120.recap: Jim slips away from the stockade and paddles out into the dark in Ben Gunn's tiny coracle.

120.label: Israel Hands
120.recap: Alone on the drifting schooner with the treacherous coxswain, Jim fights for his life in the rigging.

120.label: The Fall of a Chieftain
120.recap: At the empty treasure cache the mutineers turn on Silver, and rescue arrives in the nick of time.

145.label: Miss Brooke
145.recap: Ardent, high-minded Dorothea Brooke longs to devote her life to some great and worthy purpose.

145.label: An Unequal Match
145.recap: The dry, middle-aged scholar Mr Casaubon courts young Dorothea, who mistakes his pedantry for greatness.

145.label: The Vincys
145.recap: We meet the charming Vincy siblings, idle Fred and vain Rosamond, and their rich old uncle Featherstone.

145.label: Lydgate's Ambitions
145.recap: The story turns to Tertius Lydgate, an idealistic young doctor newly arrived with grand hopes of reform.

145.label: Disenchantment in Rome
145.recap: On her honeymoon in Rome, Dorothea weeps, already sensing how cold and empty her marriage will be.

145.label: Fred's Debt
145.recap: Feckless Fred Vincy's unpaid debt threatens to drag down the humble Garth family who trusted him.

145.label: Waiting for Death
145.recap: As miserly old Featherstone lies dying, he tries to make Mary Garth burn one of his two wills.

145.label: Casaubon's Heart
145.recap: Lydgate gently warns Casaubon that his heart may fail at any moment, deepening the scholar's bitterness.

145.label: The Dead Hand
145.recap: Casaubon's will is found to carry a cruel clause: Dorothea loses everything if she ever marries Will Ladislaw.

145.label: Raffles
145.recap: The shady past of the pious banker Bulstrode resurfaces when the blackmailer Raffles turns up in town.

145.label: A Temptation
145.recap: With Raffles dying and Lydgate sunk in debt, Bulstrode faces a terrible temptation.

145.label: The Scandal
145.recap: An ugly scandal engulfs Bulstrode and drags the innocent Lydgate's good name down with it.

145.label: A Helping Hand
145.recap: Alone among the town, Dorothea refuses to believe the worst and reaches out to the ruined Lydgate.

145.label: Sunset and Sunrise
145.recap: After much heartache and misunderstanding, Dorothea and Will are at last brought together.

161.label: The Dashwoods
161.recap: When Mr Dashwood dies, his selfish son and daughter-in-law leave the widow and her three daughters poor and unwelcome at Norland.

161.label: Elinor and Edward
161.recap: Sensible Elinor grows quietly attached to the shy Edward Ferrars, even as the family must give up their home.

161.label: Barton Cottage
161.recap: The Dashwoods settle into a modest cottage under the hearty Sir John Middleton's wing.

161.label: Willoughby
161.recap: Romantic Marianne is carried home from the rain by the dashing young Willoughby and falls headlong in love.

161.label: Willoughby Leaves
161.recap: Willoughby abruptly leaves for London, giving the heartbroken Marianne no explanation at all.

161.label: Lucy's Secret
161.recap: The sly Lucy Steele confides to Elinor that she is secretly engaged to Edward, a blow Elinor must bear in silence.

161.label: London
161.recap: In London Marianne anxiously seeks Willoughby, only to be coldly cut by him at a party.

161.label: Willoughby's Betrayal
161.recap: Willoughby returns Marianne's letters and reveals his engagement to a rich heiress, leaving her devastated.

161.label: Edward Exposed
161.recap: Edward's secret engagement to Lucy comes to light, and his furious mother disinherits him.

161.label: Willoughby's Confession
161.recap: During Marianne's grave illness a repentant Willoughby appears to pour out his excuses to Elinor.

161.label: Marianne Recovers
161.recap: Marianne recovers and resolves to live more wisely, while word comes that Lucy has married a Ferrars.

161.label: Edward Free
161.recap: It was Edward's brother whom Lucy wed; freed of his promise, Edward hurries to Elinor and proposes.

161.label: Two Marriages
161.recap: Elinor marries Edward, and in time Marianne gives her heart to the steadfast Colonel Brandon.

174.label: The Portrait
174.recap: Painter Basil completes a portrait of the beautiful young Dorian Gray while his friend Lord Henry preaches a creed of pleasure.

174.label: A Fatal Wish
174.recap: Intoxicated by Lord Henry's words, Dorian wishes the portrait would age in his place while he stays forever young.

174.label: Sibyl Vane
174.recap: Dorian falls headlong for a poor young actress, Sibyl Vane, dazzled by her talent on the stage.

174.label: Prince Charming
174.recap: Sibyl glows with love for the gentleman she calls her Prince Charming, as her brother James swears to protect her.

174.label: A Cruelty
174.recap: When Sibyl acts badly, a disgusted Dorian casts her off, and that night the painted face shows its first cruel change.

174.label: The Secret Hidden
174.recap: Learning Sibyl has died, Dorian hardens his heart and locks the changing portrait away out of sight.

174.label: Basil's Worry
174.recap: Basil pleads to understand Dorian's new coldness, but Dorian guards his terrible secret.

174.label: Years of Excess
174.recap: Dorian sinks into years of secret vice while his face stays unmarked and the hidden portrait grows monstrous.

174.label: Basil's Last Visit
174.recap: Basil corners Dorian about the rumours; Dorian shows him the corrupted portrait, and the night ends in murder.

174.label: The Opium Den
174.recap: Haunted and craving escape, Dorian visits an opium den, where Sibyl's vengeful brother nearly catches him.

174.label: The Portrait's Verdict
174.recap: Hoping to be good, Dorian turns a knife on the hideous portrait, and meets the fate it had borne for him.

215.label: Into the Primitive
215.recap: Buck, a pampered ranch dog, is stolen and sold north into the brutal world of sled dogs in the Klondike gold rush.

215.label: The Law of Club and Fang
215.recap: Buck learns the harsh law of club and fang and the savagery needed to survive among the sled teams.

215.label: The Dominant Primordial Beast
215.recap: The wild grows strong in Buck as he battles the cruel lead dog Spitz for mastery of the team.

215.label: The Toil of Trace and Trail
215.recap: Sold to greenhorns who cannot manage the trail, Buck and the worn team are driven to the edge of death.

215.label: For the Love of a Man
215.recap: Rescued by the kind John Thornton, Buck loves him fiercely and performs astonishing feats for his sake.

215.label: The Sounding of the Call
215.recap: With Thornton gone, nothing binds Buck to men any longer, and he answers the wild at last to run with the wolves.

244.label: Watson Meets Holmes
244.recap: Invalided home from war, Dr Watson is introduced to the eccentric Sherlock Holmes and they take rooms together at Baker Street.

244.label: The Science of Deduction
244.recap: Watson puzzles over his strange flatmate until Holmes reveals he is the world's first consulting detective.

244.label: The Lauriston Gardens Mystery
244.recap: Holmes is called to an empty house where a man lies dead without a wound, the word RACHE scrawled in blood on the wall.

244.label: What John Rance Had to Tell
244.recap: Holmes questions the constable who found the body and astonishes the police with deductions about the killer.

244.label: An Unexpected Visitor
244.recap: A newspaper advertisement lures someone to Baker Street, but the wily suspect slips through Holmes's fingers.

244.label: Light in the Darkness
244.recap: A second murder shakes the baffled police, and Holmes calmly announces he already has the culprit in hand.

244.label: The Great Alkali Plain
244.recap: The story leaps to the American West, where a man and a little girl, lone survivors in the desert, are rescued by Mormon pioneers.

244.label: The Flower of Utah
244.recap: Years on, the girl Lucy grows up and falls in love with a young hunter, against the elders' rigid wishes.

244.label: Ferrier and the Prophet
244.recap: The Mormon leader demands Lucy marry within the faith, and her adoptive father quietly defies him.

244.label: A Flight for Life
244.recap: Lucy, her father, and her lover flee across the mountains, hunted by the vengeful Avenging Angels.

244.label: The Avenging Angels
244.recap: Tragedy overtakes the fugitives, setting the sole survivor on a lifelong road of revenge.

244.label: The Conclusion
244.recap: Back in London the captured killer tells how the western tragedy drove his hunt, and Holmes lays out the full solution.

345.label: The Journey East
345.recap: Solicitor Jonathan Harker travels deep into the Carpathians to help a mysterious Count with a London property purchase.

345.label: A Prisoner in the Castle
345.recap: Harker realises the castle is a prison and begins to grasp the monstrous nature of his host.

345.label: Mina and Lucy
345.recap: Back in England, Mina exchanges letters with her dear friend Lucy, who is giddy over three marriage proposals.

345.label: Whitby
345.recap: Mina joins Lucy at the seaside town of Whitby, fretting over Jonathan's long silence.

345.label: The Derelict Ship
345.recap: A fierce storm drives a derelict ship ashore at Whitby, its dead captain lashed to the helm and no living soul aboard.

345.label: Sleepwalking
345.recap: Mina rescues the sleepwalking Lucy from the cliff at night and notices two small marks at her throat.

345.label: A Wasting Illness
345.recap: Lucy grows pale and weak from an illness no one can explain, and Dr Seward sends for his old teacher Van Helsing.

345.label: A Turn for the Worse
345.recap: Despite the doctors' desperate efforts, Lucy's strange decline takes a frightening turn.

345.label: Van Helsing's Warning
345.recap: Van Helsing struggles to make the sceptical Seward believe the dreadful truth about what now stalks the night.

345.label: The Churchyard
345.recap: At midnight the men keep a grim vigil at the tomb to face the horror Van Helsing has foretold.

345.label: The Hunters Unite
345.recap: The friends band together under Van Helsing and resolve to hunt their enemy to the end.

345.label: In the Asylum
345.recap: Seward's strange patient Renfield reveals what he has seen as the danger reaches into the asylum itself.

345.label: The Last Pursuit
345.recap: The hunters race across the frozen Carpathians for a final reckoning before the sun goes down.

394.label: The Amazons
394.recap: The genteel ladies who rule the little town of Cranford are scandalised by the plain-spoken newcomer Captain Brown.

394.label: Captain Brown
394.recap: Kindly Captain Brown wins the town's affection before a sudden tragedy on the railway.

394.label: An Old Love
394.recap: We learn of gentle Miss Matty's long-lost romance with the farmer Mr Holbrook.

394.label: Mr Holbrook
394.recap: A visit to Mr Holbrook's farm stirs old feelings the years had never quite stilled.

394.label: Old Letters
394.recap: Miss Matty and the narrator spend a quiet evening reading the family's bundle of old letters.

394.label: Poor Peter
394.recap: We hear how Miss Matty's mischievous brother Peter played one prank too many and ran away to sea.

394.label: Visiting
394.recap: Cranford flutters over the proper etiquette of who may call upon the grand Mrs Jamieson.

394.label: Your Ladyship
394.recap: The ladies are agog at the arrival of the titled but unassuming Lady Glenmire.

394.label: Signor Brunoni
394.recap: A travelling magician dazzles and unsettles the town, setting off a wave of nervous rumours.

394.label: The Panic
394.recap: Fear of robbers throws the timid ladies of Cranford into a delicious panic.

394.label: Old Friends
394.recap: Lady Glenmire surprises everyone, and the mystery of Signor Brunoni is gently solved.

394.label: A Clue
394.recap: A surprising engagement sets tongues wagging, and a clue surfaces about Miss Matty's long-lost brother.

394.label: Stopped Payment
394.recap: The failure of the town bank leaves the trusting Miss Matty suddenly penniless.

394.label: Friends in Need
394.recap: Her friends quietly rally round, and Miss Matty makes a brave new start selling tea.

394.label: A Happy Return
394.recap: A long-awaited return from distant lands brings joy back to Miss Matty's door.

394.label: Peace to Cranford
394.recap: With old wounds healed, gentle peace settles once more over the little town.

768.label: Lockwood's Visit
768.recap: The new tenant Lockwood calls on his sullen landlord Heathcliff at the bleak, windswept Wuthering Heights.

768.label: A Ghost at the Window
768.recap: Snowbound overnight, Lockwood is terrified by a dream of a ghost child at the window begging to be let in.

768.label: The Foundling
768.recap: Housekeeper Nelly tells how old Earnshaw's adopted foundling Heathcliff and his daughter Catherine grew inseparable, to Hindley's jealousy.

768.label: Catherine and the Lintons
768.recap: Catherine returns from the refined Lintons changed, torn between wild Heathcliff and gentle Edgar Linton.

768.label: Heathcliff Vanishes
768.recap: Overhearing Catherine say it would degrade her to marry him, Heathcliff disappears into the storm.

768.label: His Return
768.recap: Years later, with Catherine now married to Edgar, a hardened and wealthy Heathcliff suddenly returns.

768.label: Catherine's Decline
768.recap: Caught between the two men, Catherine sinks into a feverish decline as Heathcliff schemes against the Lintons.

768.label: Catherine's Death
768.recap: Catherine dies giving birth to a daughter, leaving Heathcliff wild with grief and a desperate vow.

768.label: The Next Generation
768.recap: Years on, Catherine's daughter Cathy is drawn toward the Heights and Heathcliff's sickly son Linton.

768.label: Trapped at the Heights
768.recap: Heathcliff imprisons young Cathy and forces her to marry Linton so he can seize both family estates.

768.label: Lockwood Returns
768.recap: A year later Lockwood comes back to find young Cathy and the rough Hareton quietly drawn together.

768.label: Heathcliff's End
768.recap: Worn out by his obsession, Heathcliff stops eating and dies, longing only to rejoin his Catherine at last.

844.label: Act I: Bunburying
844.recap: In his London flat Algernon needles his friend Jack about a double life, just as a marriage proposal sets a comic deception spinning.

844.label: Act II: The Country
844.recap: At the country manor two young women each believe themselves engaged to the same imaginary man named Ernest.

844.label: Act III: Earnest at Last
844.recap: The tangle of names and false identities is untangled, revealing the truth behind the name Ernest.

1184.label: Homecoming
1184.recap: The young sailor Edmond Dantès reaches Marseilles with everything before him: a captaincy promised and Mercédès to marry.

1184.label: The Conspiracy
1184.recap: Envious of his good fortune, Dantès's rivals plot to denounce him with an anonymous letter as a Bonapartist traitor.

1184.label: The Betrothal Feast
1184.recap: At his betrothal feast Dantès is seized by gendarmes and torn from Mercédès before they can wed.

1184.label: The Magistrate
1184.recap: To protect a secret that would ruin his own family, the magistrate Villefort condemns the innocent Dantès to a hidden cell.

1184.label: The Château d'If
1184.recap: Long years pass in the island fortress, where the forgotten Dantès sinks toward despair.

1184.label: The Abbé Faria
1184.recap: Dantès befriends a wise old prisoner, the Abbé Faria, who tunnels into his cell and becomes his teacher.

1184.label: The Hidden Treasure
1184.recap: The dying abbé entrusts Dantès with the secret of a vast fortune buried on the lonely isle of Monte Cristo.

1184.label: The Escape
1184.recap: Dantès makes a desperate bid for freedom, taking a dead man's place to escape the prison at last.

1184.label: Treasure of Monte Cristo
1184.recap: Free and alone, Dantès reaches the island and unearths the abbé's staggering hoard of gold and jewels.

1184.label: Tracing the Traitors
1184.recap: Disguised as a priest, Dantès quietly seeks out old acquaintances to learn exactly who betrayed him.

1184.label: The Count Appears
1184.recap: Years later in Italy a mysterious and fabulously rich Count of Monte Cristo dazzles two young Parisian travellers.

1184.label: Into Paris Society
1184.recap: The Count comes to Paris and is welcomed into the homes of the very men who once destroyed Edmond Dantès.

1184.label: Haydée
1184.recap: The Count's beautiful Greek ward, Haydée, carries a secret that ties her to one of his enemies.

1184.label: The Net Tightens
1184.recap: Moving through the salons of Paris, the Count patiently turns his enemies' own sins against them.

1184.label: A Public Disgrace
1184.recap: An old betrayal is dragged into the light, and one proud enemy of the Count faces public ruin.

1184.label: Mercédès
1184.recap: Mercédès knows Edmond at last, and pleads with him over the duel now threatening her son.

1184.label: Expiation
1184.recap: One of the Count's foes is overtaken by a ruin more terrible than anything he had feared.

1184.label: Danglars' Reckoning
1184.recap: The greedy banker Danglars meets a fitting and frightening reckoning far from home.

1184.label: Wait and Hope
1184.recap: His long vengeance complete, the Count takes his leave and gives the young lovers his parting wisdom: wait and hope.

1259.label: A Half-Pistole's Effects
1259.recap: Scraping by under the new minister Mazarin, d'Artagnan is glad to have his shrewd old servant Planchet back as Paris simmers toward revolt.

1259.label: Two Angelic Faces
1259.recap: Having won the good-natured Porthos back to adventure, d'Artagnan rides on toward his next old comrade.

1259.label: The Abbé Scarron
1259.recap: At the celebrated salon of the crippled wit Scarron, all fashionable Paris gathers amid the swirl of intrigue and discontent.

1259.label: The Ferry across the Oise
1259.recap: The young Raoul, Athos's ward, sets out for the wars, hiding the grief of leaving his beloved guardian behind.

1259.label: A Letter from Charles the First
1259.recap: At a Paris convent the exiled English queen prays for her husband Charles I, and a desperate appeal for help is set in motion.

1259.label: Another Queen in Want of Help
1259.recap: Athos quietly gathers his friends as yet another threatened sovereign turns to them for rescue.

1259.label: The Interview
1259.recap: Lodged together through the city's riots, d'Artagnan and Porthos are drawn deeper into Mazarin's dangerous games.

1259.label: The Avenger
1259.recap: In England the reunited friends stand by the betrayed King Charles as the net of treachery tightens around him.

1259.label: London
1259.recap: With their plans in ruins, the four comrades ride hard toward London, fearing the worst.

1259.label: A Cornered Foe
1259.recap: The vengeful Mordaunt, cornered by the four friends, coolly watches for any chance to slip free.

1259.label: The Ambassadors
1259.recap: Back in a rain-flooded Paris, the friends pick their way through the perils of the Fronde.

1259.label: Strength and Sagacity
1259.recap: Held captive, d'Artagnan and Porthos set their wits and their strength to engineering an escape.

1260.label: Gateshead
1260.recap: Orphaned Jane suffers under her cruel aunt and cousins at Gateshead, and is locked in terror inside the red-room.

1260.label: Lowood School
1260.recap: Jane is sent to grim, hungry Lowood charity school, where she befriends the gentle, saintly Helen Burns.

1260.label: Loss at Lowood
1260.recap: A fever sweeps Lowood, and Jane loses her beloved friend Helen to illness in the night.

1260.label: Thornfield
1260.recap: Grown and trained, Jane takes a post as governess at Thornfield Hall for the ward of its absent master.

1260.label: Mr. Rochester
1260.recap: Jane and the brooding Mr Rochester grow close, and she saves him from a mysterious fire set in his bed at night.

1260.label: The House Party
1260.recap: A glittering party fills the hall, and Jane quietly aches as Rochester seems to court the beautiful Blanche Ingram.

1260.label: The Proposal
1260.recap: In the moonlit garden Rochester declares he loves Jane, not Blanche, and asks her to be his wife.

1260.label: The Wedding Halted
1260.recap: At the altar the wedding is stopped: Rochester already has a living wife, the madwoman hidden in the attic.

1260.label: Jane Flees
1260.recap: Refusing to live as his mistress, Jane tears herself from Rochester and flees Thornfield into the dark.

1260.label: Moor House
1260.recap: Starving and penniless on the moors, Jane is taken in by the kind Rivers siblings at Moor House.

1260.label: An Inheritance
1260.recap: Jane discovers she has inherited a fortune and that the Rivers are her own cousins, whom she gladly shares it with.

1260.label: St. John's Demand
1260.recap: The cold, devout St John presses Jane to marry him for his mission, until she hears Rochester's voice call across the night.

1260.label: Reader, I Married Him
1260.recap: Jane returns to find Thornfield burned and Rochester blinded but free, and the two are reunited at last.

1342.label: A Single Man of Fortune
1342.recap: Mrs Bennet is delighted that wealthy Mr Bingley has taken nearby Netherfield, and at once schemes to match him with a daughter.

1342.label: The Meryton Assembly
1342.recap: At the local ball Bingley is taken with Jane, while his proud friend Mr Darcy slights Elizabeth, who overhears him.

1342.label: At Netherfield
1342.recap: Nursing an ill Jane at Netherfield, Elizabeth trades sharp words with the scornful Miss Bingley and the watchful Mr Darcy.

1342.label: Mr. Collins Arrives
1342.recap: The pompous clergyman Mr Collins, heir to Longbourn, arrives meaning to marry one of the Bennet girls.

1342.label: The Netherfield Ball
1342.recap: At the Netherfield ball Elizabeth dances with Darcy amid mutual sparring, while her family embarrasses itself.

1342.label: Collins Proposes
1342.recap: Mr Collins proposes to Elizabeth, who refuses him flatly, to her mother's despair.

1342.label: Charlotte's Choice
1342.recap: To Elizabeth's shock, her sensible friend Charlotte accepts Mr Collins for the comfort and security he offers.

1342.label: Darcy's Proposal
1342.recap: Darcy stuns Elizabeth with a proud declaration of love; insulted, she refuses him and throws his faults in his face.

1342.label: The Letter
1342.recap: Darcy's long letter explains his hand in parting Bingley from Jane and the real truth about Wickham, shaking Elizabeth's certainties.

1342.label: Pemberley
1342.recap: Touring Darcy's grand estate, Elizabeth hears him warmly praised and meets him again, unexpectedly gentle.

1342.label: Lydia's Flight
1342.recap: Word arrives that her reckless sister Lydia has run off with Wickham, threatening the whole family's name.

1342.label: Darcy Speaks Again
1342.recap: His feelings unchanged, Darcy proposes a second time, and now Elizabeth gladly accepts him.

1342.label: Two Weddings
1342.recap: With Jane and Elizabeth both happily married, the Bennet household settles at last into contentment.

1400.label: The Convict
1400.recap: In the marsh churchyard the orphan boy Pip is seized by an escaped convict and frightened into bringing him food and a file.

1400.label: Miss Havisham
1400.recap: Pip is summoned to the decaying Satis House to play for the strange Miss Havisham and her cold, lovely ward Estella.

1400.label: Apprenticed
1400.recap: Bound apprentice to the kind blacksmith Joe, Pip grows ashamed of his humble home and pines for Estella.

1400.label: Great Expectations
1400.recap: A lawyer brings astonishing news: Pip has a secret benefactor and great expectations, and must leave for London to become a gentleman.

1400.label: London
1400.recap: In London Pip meets the formidable lawyer Jaggers and lodges with his cheerful friend Herbert Pocket.

1400.label: Estella Returns
1400.recap: Sure that Miss Havisham means Estella for him, Pip falls deeper in love despite her warning that she has no heart.

1400.label: The Benefactor
1400.recap: On a stormy night Pip's true benefactor appears: not Miss Havisham, but the convict he once helped, Magwitch.

1400.label: Magwitch's Story
1400.recap: Magwitch tells his life, revealing a link to the man who once jilted Miss Havisham, as danger closes around him.

1400.label: Satis House Burns
1400.recap: Pip forgives a remorseful, broken Miss Havisham just before her decaying house goes up in flames.

1400.label: The Trap on the Marsh
1400.recap: Lured back to the marshes, Pip is nearly murdered by the vengeful Orlick before help arrives.

1400.label: The Escape Fails
1400.recap: The plan to smuggle Magwitch out of England collapses; he is captured and badly hurt in the struggle.

1400.label: Reunion
1400.recap: Years later, humbled and changed, Pip comes home and meets Estella once more among the ruins of Satis House.

1513.label: A Street Brawl
1513.recap: The feuding Montagues and Capulets clash in Verona until the Prince warns that the next to break the peace will die; lovesick Romeo pines for another.

1513.label: The Capulet Feast
1513.recap: At the Capulets' masked ball Romeo and Juliet meet and fall in love at once, only to learn they are children of sworn enemies.

1513.label: The Balcony
1513.recap: Beneath Juliet's window the two pledge their love and resolve to marry in secret.

1513.label: Married in Secret
1513.recap: Friar Lawrence weds Romeo and Juliet, hoping their union might heal the families' feud.

1513.label: Mercutio and Tybalt
1513.recap: Tybalt kills Mercutio; a grief-stricken Romeo kills Tybalt in turn, and the Prince banishes Romeo from Verona.

1513.label: Parting at Dawn
1513.recap: Romeo flees to Mantua after their one night together, just as Juliet's father commands her to marry Paris.

1513.label: The Friar's Plan
1513.recap: Desperate, Juliet takes a potion from Friar Lawrence that will make her seem dead so she can slip away to Romeo.

1513.label: The Potion
1513.recap: Alone and afraid, Juliet drinks the potion and sinks into a deathlike sleep.

1513.label: The Tomb
1513.recap: Believing Juliet dead, Romeo drinks poison at her tomb; waking to find him gone, Juliet takes her own life, and the families at last make peace.

1661.label: A Scandal in Bohemia
1661.recap: The King of Bohemia hires Holmes to recover a compromising photograph from Irene Adler, the one woman ever to outwit him.

1661.label: The Red-Headed League
1661.recap: A pawnbroker is paid generously to copy out an encyclopaedia for a bogus society, and Holmes senses a deeper scheme.

1661.label: A Case of Identity
1661.recap: A short-sighted typist begs Holmes to find the fiancé who vanished on the very morning of their wedding.

1661.label: The Boscombe Valley Mystery
1661.recap: A young man stands accused of murdering his father, and Holmes travels west to test the damning evidence.

1661.label: The Five Orange Pips
1661.recap: A frightened client whose relatives die soon after receiving envelopes of orange pips comes to Baker Street for help.

1661.label: The Man with the Twisted Lip
1661.recap: A respectable gentleman seems to vanish inside an opium den, and Holmes sets out to learn what became of him.

1661.label: The Blue Carbuncle
1661.recap: A precious jewel turns up inside a Christmas goose, sending Holmes on a festive chase to trace its journey.

1661.label: The Speckled Band
1661.recap: A terrified woman fears for her life after her sister died gasping of a speckled band, and Holmes eyes her sinister stepfather.

1661.label: The Engineer's Thumb
1661.recap: A hydraulic engineer arrives maimed and shaken after a nightmarish night's work for a mysterious client.

1661.label: The Noble Bachelor
1661.recap: A lord's bride disappears from the wedding breakfast, and Holmes is asked to explain her sudden flight.

1661.label: The Beryl Coronet
1661.recap: A banker entrusted with a priceless coronet finds it damaged and his own son seemingly to blame.

1661.label: The Copper Beeches
1661.recap: A governess offered a strange post, asked to cut her hair and sit at a window, asks Holmes whether she is in danger.

1695.label: The Two Poets
1695.recap: In a London suburb the poet of order, Gabriel Syme, clashes with the red-haired anarchist poet Lucian Gregory.

1695.label: The Secret of Gabriel Syme
1695.recap: Gregory secretly leads Syme to an underground anarchist meeting, never guessing Syme is an undercover detective.

1695.label: Elected Thursday
1695.recap: Syme boldly gets himself elected to the anarchist Central Council under the code name Thursday.

1695.label: The Tale of a Detective
1695.recap: Syme recalls how a man in a pitch-dark room recruited him into a secret philosophical police force.

1695.label: The Feast of Fear
1695.recap: Syme sits in dread at the Council's table beneath its vast, terrifying leader, the man called Sunday.

1695.label: The Exposure
1695.recap: Syme and the sinister Professor de Worms watch one another warily, each fearing the other has seen through him.

1695.label: The Professor Explains
1695.recap: The Professor reveals he too is an undercover detective, and the council's terror starts to come apart.

1695.label: The Duel
1695.recap: Syme fights a strange, almost unkillable duel with a council member who turns out to be yet another ally.

1695.label: The Earth in Anarchy
1695.recap: One by one the anarchists are unmasked as fellow detectives, all fleeing a mob that seems to be the whole world.

1695.label: The Pursuit of Sunday
1695.recap: The detectives give chase to the elusive, godlike Sunday across the country in an ever wilder pursuit.

1695.label: The Accuser
1695.recap: In a dreamlike finale Sunday reveals himself, and Syme faces the strange meaning behind the whole ordeal.

1727.label: The Gods in Council
1727.recap: The poet calls on the Muse; the gods agree it is time for long-lost Ulysses to be freed from Calypso's island and brought home.

1727.label: Calypso
1727.recap: Released at last by the nymph Calypso, Ulysses builds a raft and sails, only to be wrecked by Poseidon's storm.

1727.label: Nausicaa
1727.recap: Cast ashore, Ulysses is discovered by the kind princess Nausicaa, who sends him on toward her father's palace.

1727.label: The Cyclops
1727.recap: Ulysses tells how he blinded the man-eating Cyclops Polyphemus to escape his cave, drawing down Poseidon's wrath.

1727.label: Circe
1727.recap: After the wind-god's bag and the cannibal Laestrygonians, Ulysses outwits the enchantress Circe, who turns men to swine.

1727.label: The Visit to the Dead
1727.recap: Ulysses sails to the edge of the underworld to question the prophet Tiresias and meet the shades of the dead.

1727.label: The Sirens and Scylla
1727.recap: Past the Sirens and the monsters Scylla and Charybdis, his starving crew doom themselves by eating the Sun-god's cattle.

1727.label: Return to Ithaca
1727.recap: The Phaeacians carry the sleeping Ulysses home to Ithaca, where Minerva hides him in the shape of an old beggar.

1727.label: The Swineherd
1727.recap: In disguise Ulysses is taken in by his faithful old swineherd Eumaeus, who does not know his master.

1727.label: Father and Son
1727.recap: Ulysses reveals himself to his son Telemachus, and together they plot against the suitors plaguing his house.

1727.label: The Trial of the Bow
1727.recap: Penelope sets the contest of the great bow, which none of the suitors can string, that only Ulysses can win.

1727.label: The Killing of the Suitors
1727.recap: Ulysses throws off his disguise and, with Telemachus, slays the suitors and reclaims his home and his queen.

2465.label: The Stranger
2465.recap: While roaming the Andalusian wilds in search of an ancient battlefield, the narrator befriends a hunted, dangerous stranger.

2465.label: Carmen
2465.recap: In Cordova the narrator meets a bewitching gypsy named Carmen, who reads his fortune.

2465.label: Don José's Tale
2465.recap: Condemned to die, the soldier Don José tells how his consuming love for Carmen led him to ruin.

2465.label: On the Roma
2465.recap: A closing essay reflects on the language and customs of the Romany people.

2542.label: Act I: Christmas Eve
2542.recap: On Christmas Eve, Nora Helmer hides the secret debt she once took on to save her husband's life.

2542.label: Act II: The Tarantella
2542.recap: As blackmail closes in, Nora desperately tries to keep her husband away from the letterbox.

2542.label: Act III: The Reckoning
2542.recap: The truth comes out at last, forcing Nora to confront what her marriage has really been.

2554.label: The Plan
2554.recap: In stifling Petersburg the destitute ex-student Raskolnikov nurses a monstrous idea and slips out to rehearse it at a pawnbroker's.

2554.label: The Family's Burden
2554.recap: A letter reveals his sister Dounia means to marry a smug benefactor to save the family, sharpening Raskolnikov's torment.

2554.label: The Murder
2554.recap: Raskolnikov kills the old pawnbroker with an axe, and then her meek sister Lizaveta, who walks in on him.

2554.label: Fever and Dread
2554.recap: Wracked by fever, Raskolnikov hides the stolen goods and lurches between terror and reckless self-betrayal.

2554.label: Marmeladov's Death
2554.recap: He sees the drunkard Marmeladov crushed by a carriage and helps the grieving family, meeting the gentle Sonia.

2554.label: First Duel with Porfiry
2554.recap: Raskolnikov fences warily with the sly investigator Porfiry, who seems to read his every nerve.

2554.label: Sonia and Lazarus
2554.recap: Drawn to Sonia's faith and suffering, Raskolnikov has her read aloud the raising of Lazarus.

2554.label: The Second Duel
2554.recap: A second, crueler interview tightens Porfiry's psychological net around Raskolnikov.

2554.label: Confession to Sonia
2554.recap: Unable to bear it alone, Raskolnikov confesses the murders to Sonia, who begs him to give himself up.

2554.label: Porfiry's Visit
2554.recap: Porfiry comes to Raskolnikov, quietly reveals he knows the truth, and urges him to confess for his own soul.

2554.label: Svidrigailov's End
2554.recap: The corrupt Svidrigailov, his last schemes collapsed, drifts through a grim final night to its end.

2554.label: Giving Himself Up
2554.recap: Taking Sonia's cross, Raskolnikov walks to the police and confesses to the murders.

2554.label: Siberia
2554.recap: In a Siberian prison camp, with Sonia faithfully beside him, Raskolnikov begins at last to find his way toward renewal.

2600.label: Anna Pávlovna's Soirée
2600.recap: At a glittering Petersburg soirée in 1805, society chatters about Napoleon as we meet the awkward, illegitimate Pierre.

2600.label: First Taste of War
2600.recap: In Austria the army marches, and young Nicholas Rostóv meets the chaos and thrill of his first battle.

2600.label: The Sky of Austerlitz
2600.recap: Fallen wounded at Austerlitz, Prince Andrew gazes up at the vast sky and sees how small his dreams of glory were.

2600.label: Home in Moscow
2600.recap: Nicholas comes home on leave to the warm, chaotic, loving Rostóv household in Moscow.

2600.label: A Search for Meaning
2600.recap: His marriage to Hélène in ruins, the restless Pierre seeks a purpose and is drawn into Freemasonry.

2600.label: Andrew Reawakened
2600.recap: A glimpse of the joyful young Natásha rouses Prince Andrew from his grief and back toward life.

2600.label: Natásha's First Ball
2600.recap: At her first grand ball Natásha is wretched with nerves until Prince Andrew chooses her to dance.

2600.label: Temptation
2600.recap: With Andrew away, the trusting Natásha is dazzled and nearly destroyed by the heartless Anatole.

2600.label: The Comet
2600.recap: Full of tender new feeling for Natásha, Pierre looks up into the night at the great comet of 1812.

2600.label: The Invasion
2600.recap: Napoleon's vast army crosses into Russia, and the whole country is swept toward war.

2600.label: Borodinó
2600.recap: On the great day of Borodinó, Pierre wanders out as a curious onlooker and finds himself caught in the thick of the slaughter at the Raévski redoubt.

2600.label: Moscow Abandoned
2600.recap: The people flee, and Moscow is left empty and silent to the advancing French.

2600.label: A Prisoner
2600.recap: A captive of the French, Pierre finds unexpected peace in the simple faith of the peasant Platón Karatáev.

2600.label: Seven Years On
2600.recap: Years later the survivors have built quieter lives, deeply changed by all they lived through.

2641.label: The Bertolini
2641.recap: In a Florence pension Lucy and her fretful chaperon Charlotte are unhappy with their rooms, until the blunt Mr Emerson and his son offer to swap.

2641.label: In Santa Croce
2641.recap: Exploring a church without her guidebook, Lucy falls in with the kindly, unconventional Mr Emerson and his quiet son George.

2641.label: A Death in the Piazza
2641.recap: A sudden killing in the square sends Lucy fainting into George's arms, an encounter that quietly unsettles her.

2641.label: Among the Violets
2641.recap: On a country drive Lucy comes upon George on a hillside of violets, and he kisses her before Charlotte sweeps her away.

2641.label: They Return
2641.recap: Charlotte hurries Lucy out of Florence, and the two women bury the kiss in careful silence.

2641.label: Back at Windy Corner
2641.recap: Home in England, Lucy accepts a proposal from the polished, controlling Cecil Vyse.

2641.label: Cecil's Scheme
2641.recap: Amused by his country neighbours, Cecil mischievously arranges for two strangers to take a nearby villa.

2641.label: The Sacred Lake
2641.recap: George, it turns out, is the new neighbour; he joins Freddy and Mr Beebe for a wild, laughing bathe in the woodland pond.

2641.label: The Disaster Within
2641.recap: George kisses Lucy once more and declares his love; flustered, she orders him away as her engagement starts to feel wrong.

2641.label: Lying to George
2641.recap: Lucy sends George off for good, refusing to admit, even to herself, what she truly feels.

2641.label: Lying to Cecil
2641.recap: Seeing Cecil clearly at last, Lucy breaks off the engagement.

2641.label: Lying to Mr. Emerson
2641.recap: About to flee abroad to escape the muddle, Lucy meets old Mr Emerson, who gently makes her face the truth of her heart.

2641.label: A Room with a View
2641.recap: Lucy and George return to the Florence pension as newlyweds, the long muddle finally undone.

2701.label: Loomings
2701.recap: Restless and gloomy on land, Ishmael decides the cure is to go to sea, this time on a whaling voyage.

2701.label: The Spouter-Inn
2701.recap: At a crowded New Bedford inn Ishmael must share a bed with a fearsome, tattooed harpooneer, Queequeg.

2701.label: Bosom Friends
2701.recap: Ishmael and the noble cannibal Queequeg become fast friends and resolve to ship out together.

2701.label: The Pequod
2701.recap: In Nantucket the two sign aboard the Pequod, a weathered whaler whose captain, Ahab, stays strangely unseen.

2701.label: The Prophet
2701.recap: A ragged stranger, Elijah, darkly hints at the doom awaiting any crew that sails under Captain Ahab.

2701.label: Ahab
2701.recap: Days out to sea, Ahab at last appears on deck: grim, scarred, standing on a leg of carved whale ivory.

2701.label: The Quarter-Deck
2701.recap: Ahab nails a gold doubloon to the mast and binds the crew by oath to hunt one creature: the white whale, Moby Dick.

2701.label: The Whiteness of the Whale
2701.recap: Ishmael tells the legend of Moby Dick, the vast white whale that took Ahab's leg and now owns his soul.

2701.label: The First Lowering
2701.recap: At the first hunt Ahab unveils his own hidden boat-crew, and a squall nearly drowns Ishmael's whaleboat.

2701.label: The Castaway
2701.recap: The little cabin-boy Pip is left adrift in the sea; rescued at last, he is never the same, his wits set loose.

2701.label: The Try-Works
2701.recap: Rendering blubber by firelight at night, Ishmael is nearly hypnotized into steering the ship to ruin.

2701.label: Queequeg's Coffin
2701.recap: Gravely ill, Queequeg has a coffin built, then recovers; the box is later sealed into a spare life-buoy.

2701.label: The Chase, First Day
2701.recap: Moby Dick is sighted at last, and on the first day of the chase the white whale shatters Ahab's boat.

2701.label: The Chase, Second Day
2701.recap: The hunt rages on; the whale wrecks more boats and Ahab's shadowy harpooneer Fedallah is dragged under.

2701.label: The Chase, Third Day
2701.recap: On the third day Moby Dick staves in the Pequod itself; the ship goes down with all hands, Ahab among them.

2852.label: Mr. Sherlock Holmes
2852.recap: Holmes and Watson examine a walking-stick left by Dr Mortimer and deduce a great deal before the visitor returns.

2852.label: The Curse of the Baskervilles
2852.recap: Mortimer reads the old legend of a demonic hound said to haunt and hunt the Baskerville family.

2852.label: The Problem
2852.recap: Mortimer recounts Sir Charles Baskerville's mysterious death, with the footprints of a gigantic hound found nearby.

2852.label: Sir Henry Baskerville
2852.recap: The young heir Sir Henry arrives in London, already being shadowed and sent an anonymous warning to stay away.

2852.label: Baskerville Hall
2852.recap: Watson escorts Sir Henry to the gloomy hall on the moor, where an escaped convict is also at large.

2852.label: The Stapletons
2852.recap: Watson meets the naturalist Stapleton and his striking sister, who strangely begs Sir Henry to leave the moor.

2852.label: Watson's Watch
2852.recap: Settled at the hall, Watson tracks odd happenings: a sobbing in the night and the butler signalling across the dark.

2852.label: The Man on the Tor
2852.recap: Trailing a stranger to a stone hut on the moor, Watson discovers it is Holmes himself, secretly at work.

2852.label: Death on the Moor
2852.recap: A body is found on the rocks; to their relief it is the convict, and Holmes prepares to spring his trap.

2852.label: The Hound
2852.recap: Out of the fog the monstrous glowing hound attacks Sir Henry, and Holmes shoots it down and exposes the killer.

2852.label: A Retrospection
2852.recap: In London Holmes explains how Stapleton, a hidden Baskerville heir, used a painted hound to murder for the estate.

2868.label: The Lovers
2868.recap: The young lovers Lucy and Archie spar over a thousand-pound bargain tangled up with their hopes of marriage.

2868.label: The Unexpected
2868.recap: Archie frets that Professor Braddock's consent to the match was bought, uneasy at the old man's hunger for money.

2868.label: The Inquest
2868.recap: The sleepy village of Gartley wakes to sudden fame as police and reporters descend to inquire into a shocking crime.

2868.label: Mrs. Jasher's Luck
2868.recap: Weeks pass and interest in the unsolved murder of Braddock's assistant cools to idle village gossip.

2868.label: The Manuscript
2868.recap: Talk keeps returning to the vanished green mummy that the dead man had brought to England.

2868.label: The Unexpected Happens
2868.recap: Fresh rumours race through the village, and an unexpected development reopens the case.

2868.label: Circumstantial Evidence
2868.recap: Professor Braddock flings a fierce accusation at the young soldier Random, who is baffled by the charge.

2868.label: Nearer the Truth
2868.recap: A startling recognition of one of the men present brings the tangled mystery a step nearer its solution.

2868.label: Just in Time
2868.recap: One of the missing emeralds is returned anonymously, the strangest twist yet in the affair of the green mummy.

2868.label: A Confession
2868.recap: A night-time alarm and a terrified servant give way at last to a long-withheld confession.

2868.label: By the River
2868.recap: After a long day's manhunt the pursuers gather for a secret meeting down by the river.

3011.label: Canto I: The Chase
3011.recap: A royal stag-hunt in the Highlands leads a lost huntsman to a lovely maiden, Ellen, beside a lonely lake.

3011.label: Canto II: The Island
3011.recap: On Ellen's island we meet her father, the outlawed Douglas, and the fierce chieftain Roderick Dhu who loves her.

3011.label: Canto III: The Gathering
3011.recap: Roderick Dhu raises the clans for war, sending the Fiery Cross flaming across the glens.

3011.label: Canto IV: The Prophecy
3011.recap: A seer's grim prophecy hangs over the gathering, while Ellen's heart turns to the gallant stranger Fitz-James.

3011.label: Canto V: The Combat
3011.recap: Fitz-James and Roderick Dhu meet as foes, and a fierce single combat decides much.

3011.label: Canto VI: The Guard-Room
3011.recap: All paths lead at last to the king's court, where the true identity of Fitz-James is revealed.

3268.label: La Vallée
3268.recap: In a peaceful corner of Gascony, the gentle Emily St. Aubert grows up devoted to her parents and to nature.

3268.label: A Mother Lost
3268.recap: Emily's mother dies, and her grieving father takes her travelling through the mountains for his failing health.

3268.label: Valancourt
3268.recap: On the journey a tender attachment grows between Emily and the spirited young Valancourt.

3268.label: Alone in the World
3268.recap: Far from home, Emily's beloved father dies, leaving her an orphan.

3268.label: Madame Cheron
3268.recap: Emily is taken in by her cold and vain aunt, Madame Cheron.

3268.label: Montoni
3268.recap: Madame Cheron marries the sinister Italian Montoni, and the household is carried off toward Venice.

3268.label: The Castle of Udolpho
3268.recap: Montoni shuts them up in his brooding, half-ruined castle deep in the Apennines.

3268.label: Whispers in the Castle
3268.recap: Servants' tales of ghosts and a dreadful secret behind a black veil prey on Emily's mind.

3268.label: Under Siege
3268.recap: Montoni fills the castle with rough soldiers as his schemes turn openly violent.

3268.label: Montoni's Tyranny
3268.recap: Standing alone, Emily resists Montoni's threats and his demands that she sign away her estates.

3268.label: Château-le-Blanc
3268.recap: The scene shifts to a haunted château in Languedoc and its kindly new owner, the Count De Villefort.

3268.label: A New Refuge
3268.recap: Having escaped Udolpho, Emily finds shelter at the château among new friends.

3268.label: Montoni's Fate
3268.recap: Word arrives of the reckoning that has at last overtaken the tyrant Montoni.

3268.label: The North Apartments
3268.recap: Strange happenings in the château's shut-up north rooms revive old fears and buried secrets.

3268.label: All Explained
3268.recap: Every lingering mystery is unravelled at last, and Emily finds her long-delayed happiness.

4085.label: Born in Scotland
4085.recap: Disowned father, mother dead young: little Roderick Random is left to the mercy of a cold grandfather and cruel relations.

4085.label: Uncle Bowling
4085.recap: His rough but warm-hearted uncle, Lieutenant Tom Bowling, arrives to rescue the neglected boy.

4085.label: Apprenticed to Surgery
4085.recap: Penniless and shunned, Roderick learns surgery under the cynical Mr Crab, who at last stakes him to seek his fortune in London.

4085.label: The Road to London
4085.recap: Roderick and his devoted friend Strap set off for London, meeting a colourful and dangerous crew of fellow travellers.

4085.label: Green in the City
4085.recap: Reaching London as raw newcomers, the pair are gulled and fleeced at every turn.

4085.label: The Apothecary's House
4085.recap: Working for the French apothecary Lavement, Roderick is caught between a scheming mother and her daughter.

4085.label: Pressed to Sea
4085.recap: Forced aboard a man of war, Roderick endures the brutal squalor of the ship's surgery in the cockpit.

4085.label: Cartagena
4085.recap: His ship joins the disastrous British assault on Cartagena, amid disease, blunder and slaughter.

4085.label: Shipwrecked
4085.recap: Homeward bound under the brutal Crampley, Roderick is wrecked on the English coast and robbed of everything.

4085.label: The Poet Melopoyn
4085.recap: Thrown into a debtors' prison, Roderick befriends the luckless poet Melopoyn and hears his tale of literary misfortune.

4085.label: Father and Fortune
4085.recap: Reunited at last with his long-lost father and his fortune made, Roderick heads home to buy back the family estate.

5200.label: The Transformation
5200.recap: Gregor Samsa wakes transformed into a monstrous insect, and his family recoils as he fails to leave for work.

5200.label: A Prisoner at Home
5200.recap: Shut in his room, Gregor adjusts to his strange body while his sister warily feeds him and the household strains.

5200.label: The End
5200.recap: Wounded and unwanted, Gregor wastes away, and his relieved family turns hopefully toward the future.

6133.label: The Arrest of Arsène Lupin
6133.recap: Aboard a transatlantic liner, word spreads that the master thief Arsène Lupin is among the passengers in disguise.

6133.label: Arsène Lupin in Prison
6133.recap: Even locked behind bars, Lupin contrives to rob a baron's riverside chateau entirely by post.

6133.label: The Escape of Arsène Lupin
6133.recap: Awaiting trial, Lupin cheerfully promises he will walk free, and sets about proving it.

6133.label: The Mysterious Traveller
6133.recap: A night train journey turns sinister when the narrator finds danger sharing his compartment.

6133.label: The Queen's Necklace
6133.recap: A famous diamond necklace vanishes from a locked room, baffling its owners for years.

6133.label: The Seven of Hearts
6133.recap: The narrator recounts the curious affair of a playing card that first drew him into Lupin's orbit.

6133.label: Madame Imbert's Safe
6133.recap: Lupin sets his sights on the fortune said to be locked away in Madame Imbert's safe.

6133.label: The Black Pearl
6133.recap: A priceless black pearl draws Lupin into a midnight burglary that does not go to plan.

6133.label: Sherlock Holmes Arrives Too Late
6133.recap: A great English detective is summoned to a country chateau, only to find Lupin a step ahead.

6593.label: The Foundling
6593.recap: The good Squire Allworthy comes home to find a baby asleep in his bed, and resolves to raise the foundling as his own son.

6593.label: Tom and Blifil
6593.recap: Tom grows into a warm but reckless youth, while the demure Master Blifil quietly turns the household against him.

6593.label: Sophia Western
6593.recap: Tom and Sophia, the neighbouring squire's lovely daughter, fall in love.

6593.label: A Forbidden Match
6593.recap: Squire Western dreams of marrying Sophia to Blifil, never guessing that her heart already belongs to Tom.

6593.label: Cast Out
6593.recap: Turned out of doors through Blifil's scheming, Tom sets off to seek his fortune, while Sophia too flees her father's house.

6593.label: On the Road
6593.recap: On his wanderings Tom gains a faithful companion in the barber Partridge and hears the strange tale of the Man of the Hill.

6593.label: The Inn at Upton
6593.recap: At the inn at Upton a tangle of mistaken rooms and jealousies sends Tom and Sophia past each other in the night.

6593.label: Sophia's Flight
6593.recap: Sophia presses on toward London with her maid, nursing her hurt at Tom's wandering ways.

6593.label: London
6593.recap: In London Tom is drawn into the worldly Lady Bellaston's orbit, even as he longs only for Sophia.

6593.label: Sophia Confined
6593.recap: Sophia's father and a scheming lord press her toward a marriage she does not want.

6593.label: The Lowest Ebb
6593.recap: With Tom flung into prison and seemingly ruined, his few true friends despair of saving him.

6593.label: All Revealed
6593.recap: At the darkest hour the secret of Tom's birth comes to light, Blifil's villainy is exposed, and fortune turns at last.

8492.label: The Repairer of Reputations
8492.recap: In a strange near-future New York, a man unhinged by a forbidden play nurses a secret claim to a throne.

8492.label: The Mask
8492.recap: A sculptor discovers a fluid that turns living things to marble, amid a love triangle shadowed by the cursed play.

8492.label: In the Court of the Dragon
8492.recap: Having read the dreaded play, a man feels himself pursued through a Paris church by a sinister organist.

8492.label: The Yellow Sign
8492.recap: A painter and his model are drawn toward horror by a corpse-like churchyard watchman and a fatal symbol.

8492.label: The Demoiselle d'Ys
8492.recap: A hunter lost on the lonely moors slips into a vanished world of falconry and a lady out of the past.

8492.label: The Prophets' Paradise
8492.recap: A series of brief, dreamlike prose-poems forms a haunting interlude.

8492.label: The Street of the Four Winds
8492.recap: A stray cat leads a painter to a quiet, sorrowful discovery in a garret across the way.

8492.label: The Street of the First Shell
8492.recap: Amid the Prussian siege and bombardment of Paris, lovers cling together as the shells fall.

8492.label: The Street of Our Lady of the Fields
8492.recap: An American art student finds love in the Latin Quarter of Paris.

8492.label: Rue Barrée
8492.recap: The art students of the Quarter vie over an unattainable young woman known to them only as Rue Barrée.

16328.label: Prologue: The Spear-Danes
16328.recap: The poem opens with the glory of the Danish kings, from the founder Scyld Scefing onward.

16328.label: Grendel
16328.recap: Night after night the monster Grendel raids King Hrothgar's hall, devouring his warriors for twelve long years.

16328.label: Beowulf Sails
16328.recap: The mighty Geat Beowulf hears of the Danes' suffering and sails across the sea to help.

16328.label: At Heorot
16328.recap: Beowulf comes before King Hrothgar and vows to face Grendel with his bare hands.

16328.label: Unferth's Taunt
16328.recap: The jealous Unferth mocks Beowulf, who answers by recounting a great swimming feat of his youth.

16328.label: The Fight with Grendel
16328.recap: That night Grendel bursts into the hall and is seized in Beowulf's terrible grip.

16328.label: Victory
16328.recap: Grendel flees mortally wounded, and the Danes rejoice and heap honours on Beowulf.

16328.label: Grendel's Mother
16328.recap: In vengeance Grendel's monstrous mother strikes the hall in the dead of night.

16328.label: The Fight in the Mere
16328.recap: Beowulf dives into the haunted lake to battle Grendel's mother in her hall beneath the water.

16328.label: The Dragon
16328.recap: Fifty years on, a thief robs an ancient hoard and wakes a dragon that burns the land.

16328.label: Beowulf and the Dragon
16328.recap: Now an old king, Beowulf arms himself once more and goes out to face the fire-drake.

16328.label: Wiglaf's Loyalty
16328.recap: As the old king falters against the dragon, only the young Wiglaf stays true and springs to his side.

16328.label: The Funeral Pyre
16328.recap: The Geats mourn their fallen king and raise a great barrow above his ashes.

16389.label: An Advertisement
16389.recap: Drab, weather-worn Mrs Wilkins spies a newspaper notice offering a small Italian castle to let for the month of April.

16389.label: A Kindred Spirit
16389.recap: She persuades the equally downtrodden Mrs Arbuthnot to share the cost and the dream of escape.

16389.label: Finding Companions
16389.recap: To afford it the two women seek two more to share, drawing the aloof Lady Caroline and the formidable Mrs Fisher.

16389.label: The Journey South
16389.recap: The two friends set off across France and Italy toward the castle of San Salvatore.

16389.label: Arrival in the Rain
16389.recap: They arrive wet, weary and in the dark, unsure quite what they have come to.

16389.label: First Morning
16389.recap: Morning reveals the castle ablaze with flowers and sea light, and the weariness begins to fall away.

16389.label: Settling In
16389.recap: The four women begin, warily, to share the beauty of the place.

16389.label: Mrs Fisher's Domain
16389.recap: Prim Mrs Fisher stakes out the best room, clinging to her dignity among strangers.

16389.label: Softening
16389.recap: The garden's gentle spell begins to thaw the guarded hearts of its visitors.

16389.label: Letters Home
16389.recap: Transformed by the place, Mrs Wilkins writes to invite the husband she had grown apart from.

16389.label: An Unexpected Guest
16389.recap: Mr Wilkins arrives, and an awkward misunderstanding turns surprisingly sweet.

16389.label: Sending for Frederick
16389.recap: Warmed and softened, Mrs Arbuthnot writes at last to summon her own estranged husband.

16389.label: Full Bloom
16389.recap: Under a full moon the garden works its final magic on every heart gathered there.

19476.label: The First Cruise of the Astronef
19476.recap: Passengers on an Atlantic liner witness the maiden flight of the Astronef, the first ship able to sail through space.

19476.label: A Courtship Aloft
19476.recap: Lord Redgrave's courtship of the American heiress Zaidie unfolds under her watchful chaperone's eye.

19476.label: The Honeymoon Begins
19476.recap: Newly wed, Redgrave and Zaidie set off from Washington on a honeymoon voyage among the worlds.

19476.label: The Moon
19476.recap: Their first landfall is the dead, airless Moon, where they explore the ruins of a world that has been.

19476.label: Toward Venus
19476.recap: The Astronef crosses a hundred million miles of space toward the cloud-wrapped planet Venus.

19476.label: The Morning Star
19476.recap: On Venus the travellers find a paradise world and marvel at its gentle inhabitants.

19476.label: Across the Asteroids
19476.recap: Crossing the belt of asteroids, the voyagers press on toward the giant planet Jupiter.

19476.label: Ganymede
19476.recap: They spend a remarkable week on Ganymede, one of Jupiter's great moons.

19476.label: Beyond Saturn
19476.recap: Far past Saturn, a sudden failure of the instruments warns of unexpected danger.

19476.label: Homeward
19476.recap: Their perilous voyage among the planets draws to a close, and the wanderers turn at last for home.

19771.label: The House of Armine
19771.recap: We learn the history of the proud but debt-burdened Armine family and the sheltered upbringing of young Ferdinand.

19771.label: A Reverie at Ducie
19771.recap: Smitten Captain Ferdinand Armine longs to stay on at Ducie, under the same roof as the lovely Henrietta Temple.

19771.label: Parting
19771.recap: The secretly betrothed lovers must part, and Ferdinand feels the deep melancholy of separation.

19771.label: Henrietta's Letter
19771.recap: Uneasy and kept in the dark, Henrietta writes begging Ferdinand to be honest about why they stay apart.

19771.label: A Slow Recovery
19771.recap: Gravely ill after the crisis, Ferdinand slowly mends under his family's anxious care.

19771.label: Lord Montfort
19771.recap: The patient, generous Lord Montfort courts the heartsick Henrietta, trusting that time will win her.

19771.label: The Value of Money
19771.recap: Hounded by debt and a match he never wanted, Ferdinand begins to see how money threatens his love.

19771.label: Count Mirabel
19771.recap: The brilliant Count Mirabel takes up Ferdinand's cause amid the glitter of fashionable London.

19771.label: A Happy Ending
19771.recap: With every obstacle swept away, Ferdinand and Henrietta are united at last with all their friends' blessing.

28054.label: The Karamazov Family
28054.recap: We meet the dissolute landowner Fyodor Pavlovitch Karamazov and his three very different sons.

28054.label: At the Monastery
28054.recap: The quarrelling family gathers at the monastery for the saintly elder Zossima to settle a dispute over money.

28054.label: Father and Son
28054.recap: Hot-tempered Dmitri arrives late, and his bitter feud with his father erupts before the elder.

28054.label: The Servants
28054.recap: Attention turns to the Karamazov household and its sly, resentful servant, the foundling Smerdyakov.

28054.label: Sent into the World
28054.recap: The dying elder blesses Alyosha and sends him out of the monastery to live among ordinary men.

28054.label: Rebellion
28054.recap: Ivan pours out his heart to Alyosha, rebelling against a God who allows the suffering of innocent children.

28054.label: The Grand Inquisitor
28054.recap: Ivan recites his poem in which Christ returns to earth and is confronted by the Grand Inquisitor.

28054.label: An Odour of Corruption
28054.recap: After the elder's death the monastery is shaken when his body decays, sorely testing Alyosha's faith.

28054.label: Mitya's Torment
28054.recap: Frantic for money and wild with jealousy over Grushenka, Dmitri rushes desperately about the town.

28054.label: At Mokroe
28054.recap: Dmitri chases Grushenka to a wild night of feasting at the village of Mokroe.

28054.label: The Investigation
28054.recap: A sudden crime brings the authorities down on Dmitri, and a long night of questioning begins.

28054.label: The Boys
28054.recap: The story pauses to follow a band of schoolboys and the proud, sickly little Ilusha.

28054.label: Ivan and Smerdyakov
28054.recap: Tormented by doubt, Ivan returns again and again to Smerdyakov, circling a terrible suspicion.

28054.label: The Trial
28054.recap: The whole district crowds into the courthouse for Dmitri's long-awaited trial.

36462.label: Merlin Foretells Arthur's Birth
36462.recap: In a Britain torn by war the boy wizard Merlin reveals his powers and foretells the coming of a great king.

36462.label: The Sword Excalibur
36462.recap: Raised in secret by Sir Ector, young Arthur draws the sword from the stone and is revealed as the true king.

36462.label: Driving Out the Saxons
36462.recap: The young king puts down the rival kings and Saxon invaders who challenge his crown.

36462.label: The King's Adventures
36462.recap: With Britain at peace, Arthur and his knights ride out in search of adventure.

36462.label: Balin and Balan
36462.recap: The brave but ill-fated Sir Balin is drawn into a tragic quarrel that ends between brothers.

36462.label: The Round Table
36462.recap: Arthur weds the fair Guinevere and gathers his finest knights at the fellowship of the Round Table.

36462.label: Arthur and Accolon
36462.recap: Treachery lures Arthur into a deadly duel against the knight Accolon of Gaul.

36462.label: Emperor at Rome
36462.recap: Arthur defies the Roman emperor Lucius's demand for tribute and marches in triumph against him.

36462.label: Sir Gawain
36462.recap: Sir Gawain rides out on adventure and learns hard lessons of courtesy and mercy.

36462.label: Sir Lancelot
36462.recap: The peerless Sir Lancelot wins fame across the land through his many bold adventures.

36462.label: Sir Gareth
36462.recap: A mocked kitchen-boy proves himself the noble knight Sir Gareth, brother to Gawain.

36462.label: Sir Tristram
36462.recap: The story turns to the gallant Sir Tristram and his ill-starred love for the fair Iseult.

36462.label: The Quest of the Grail
36462.recap: Merlin is lost to enchantment, and the knights set out on the long and holy quest for the Grail.

36462.label: Lancelot and Elaine
36462.recap: The Grail quest ended, Lancelot's deeds and his love for the fair Elaine cast long shadows over the court.

36462.label: The Passing of Arthur
36462.recap: Old jealousies tear the fellowship apart, and Arthur is borne away at last to the isle of Avalon.

37106.label: Playing Pilgrims
37106.recap: The four March sisters, poor while their father is away at war, resolve to bear their lot cheerfully one Christmas.

37106.label: A Merry Christmas
37106.recap: The girls give their Christmas breakfast to a starving family, and are rewarded by their rich neighbor Mr Laurence.

37106.label: The Laurence Boy
37106.recap: At a New Year's dance Jo strikes up a warm friendship with the Laurences' shy grandson, Laurie.

37106.label: Jo Meets Apollyon
37106.recap: Jo's hot temper flares when Amy burns her manuscript, and a near-drowning on the ice teaches Jo to forgive.

37106.label: A Telegram
37106.recap: A telegram brings word that Mr March is gravely ill, and Marmee hurries away to nurse him at the front.

37106.label: Little Faithful
